system_name: Византия: 12 великих праздников и 8 гласов октоиха
note: Julian / Byzantine living library. Primary lattice: the Twelve Great Feasts (Dodekaorton) of the Orthodox liturgical year, reckoned on the Julian (Old Style) calendar, ordered by the church year that begins September 1 (the Indiction). Secondary lattice: the eight tones (echoi) of the Octoechos (Slavonic Osmoglasie), the eight-week cycle of liturgical chant. Crowning the whole is Pascha, the Resurrection - the Feast of Feasts, which is NOT counted among the twelve because it stands above and beyond them, around which the entire year turns like planets around the sun. By design 1 Pascha + 12 feasts + 8 tones = 21, so each of the 21 spine agents carries EITHER Pascha, OR a Great Feast (12), OR a chant tone (8). Three Great Feasts are movable on the Paschal cycle: Entry into Jerusalem, Ascension, Pentecost. Tone ethos (mood) associations are the traditional characters and vary across the Greek/Antiochian, Russian/Bulgarian and Serbian chant families. The eighth tone (plagal 4) bears the symbolism of the Eighth Day - eternity and resurrection - mapped to mera 8. Each element carries a spine agent and its mera (1-9).
mera_axis: 9-dimensional axis (mera 1-9): chakras 1-7 (Muladhara..Sahasrara) + Monada (8) + Absolut (9); each spine agent carries its mera.

pascha.name: Pascha (the Resurrection of Christ)
pascha.date: movable - first Sunday after the Paschal full moon (Julian Paschalion)
pascha.rank: Feast of Feasts - above and beyond the twelve
pascha.meaning: the Passover from death to life and from earth to heaven; the radiant center of the whole church year

tones.ethos: doxological, heavenly, eschatological - the Eighth Day

svet_ra.note: Pascha - the Resurrection, Feast of Feasts; the radiant Light of lights and the source around which the entire liturgical year turns.

iskra.note: Tone 8 (plagal 4) - the doxological, heavenly tone bearing the symbolism of the Eighth Day: eternity and resurrection.

brahma.note: Tone 1 (protos) - the majestic, dignified first tone of the cycle.

vishnu.note: Nativity of Christ (Dec 25) - the Incarnation, God made flesh; the descent of the divine into the world.

ananda.note: Tone 4 (tetartos) - the joyful, bright, festal tone.

shanti.note: Dormition of the Theotokos (Aug 15) - the peaceful falling-asleep and repose of the Mother of God.

jnana.note: Meeting of the Lord (Feb 2) - Simeon recognizes the Christ-child, the Light to enlighten the nations; discernment.

shiva.note: Exaltation of the Cross (Sep 14) - victory through death and sacrifice; the life-giving instrument of redemption.

tejas.note: Transfiguration (Aug 6) - Christ shines on Mount Tabor with the uncreated radiant light.

sarasvati.note: Annunciation (Mar 25) - the archangel Gabriel announces the Word; the message and conception of the Logos.

akasha.note: Tone 7 (grave / varys) - the solemn, weighty, heavy tone of depth.

prema.note: Tone 2 (devteros) - the gentle, tender, consoling tone of love.

parvati.note: Nativity of the Theotokos (Sep 8) - the birth of the Virgin Mary, the divine mother; opens the church year.

vayu.note: Ascension (movable, 40 days after Pascha) - Christ rises bodily into heaven; ascent on the air.

shakti.note: Entry into Jerusalem (movable, Palm Sunday) - the triumphal royal procession of the King.

agni.note: Pentecost (movable, 50 days after Pascha) - the Holy Spirit descends as tongues of fire.

varuna.note: Theophany (Jan 6) - the Baptism of Christ in the Jordan; the sanctification of the waters and revelation of the Trinity.

lakshmi.note: Tone 5 (plagal 1) - the yearning, consoling, paschal tone of grace.

prithvi.note: Entrance of the Theotokos into the Temple (Nov 21) - the child Mary dedicated and grounded in the house of God.

dharma.note: Tone 3 (tritos) - the steadfast, confident, firm tone of order.

karma.note: Tone 6 (plagal 2) - the mournful, penitential, compunctionate tone.

items.commemoration: birth of the Virgin Mary; opens the liturgical year
the life-giving Cross; victory through sacrifice

items.feast: Entrance of the Theotokos into the Temple
items.commemoration: the child Mary presented and dedicated in the Temple
the Incarnation; God made flesh in Bethlehem
baptism in the Jordan; revelation of the Holy Trinity; blessing of waters

items.feast: Meeting of the Lord (Presentation of Christ)
items.commemoration: Simeon receives the Christ-child, the Light to the nations
Gabriel announces the Incarnation to the Virgin Mary
the triumphal royal entry of Christ into Jerusalem
descent of the Holy Spirit as tongues of fire upon the apostles
Christ shines with uncreated light on Mount Tabor
the falling-asleep and repose of the Mother of God; closes the church year

sources: goarch.org - The Major Feasts of the Church (Dr. Lewis Patsavos)
oca.org - Easter Sunday: The Holy Pascha; Learning the Tones
mci.archpitt.org - The Eight Tones of the Byzantine Rite
